From problem diagnosis to solution implementation
hero.microText: First step — a short discussion of your task to understand where exactly the business is losing time, money, or control.

painPoints.title: When there's data but no system
painPoints.subtitle: I help businesses transition from manual chaos and scattered spreadsheets to clear process logic, requirements, MVP, and management reporting.

items.title: Process Chaos
items.text: Employees work differently, there are no unified rules, and process weaknesses remain invisible until errors, delays, or losses appear.

items.title: Reports exist, but managing is hard
items.text: Numbers are collected manually, sources are scattered, reports don't provide a clear picture and don't help make quick decisions.

items.title: Need automation, but don't know where to start
items.text: There's a desire to digitize processes, but no schema, data structure, or clear technical specification for implementation.

items.title: Need to quickly test an idea
items.text: When it's important not to build a big system right away, but first to assemble a working MVP and test the hypothesis in practice.

items.title: Everything works in silos
items.text: Excel, Google Sheets, 1C, Power BI — separate tools exist, but there's no unified system, logic, or transparency for the business.

items.title: Need clear analytics
items.text: When the owner or manager needs not just a file with numbers, but a clear business picture for decision-making.

about.name: Kutman Omuraliev
about.title: Business, Data & Technical Implementation — All in One Package
about.shortText: Kutman Omuraliev — business analyst and BI specialist with experience at the intersection of accounting, business processes, and IT. Worked on tasks at Kumtor, management reporting, process automation, and building analytical solutions. Helping businesses transition from manual chaos to a clear management system.
about.strengthText: I approach tasks not as a technical executor, but as someone who understands how accounting and finance work, how a business process looks in reality, and how to translate a task into a clear schema, specification, and working solution.
proofPoints: Background in accounting, business analysis & BI
Power BI, SQL, Python, Excel, Draw.io
From diagnosis to implementation and support
about.quote: My goal is not just to create a report or diagram, but to build a system that the owner and team can rely on every day.

career.desc: Bug fixing, writing software code, optimizing processes.
Business process analysis, specification development, testing, documentation.

Management reporting: data consolidation, deep data analysis.
Report development, Power BI, Excel VBA. Specifications for 1C developers.

services.title: Services packaged into clear business results
services.subtitle: Not just consulting or reports, but specific work with a problem: diagnosis, logic description, solution creation, and implementation support.

roadmap.title: Clear path: from first meeting to support
roadmap.subtitle: Work is built step by step so the client understands at which stage the task is and what result comes next.

steps.name: Negotiation
steps.desc: Brief discussion of the task, context, expectations, and success criteria.

steps.name: Problem Diagnosis
steps.desc: We analyze the current situation, identify bottlenecks, errors, and growth points.

steps.name: Process Mapping
steps.desc: We document the process as clear visual logic.

steps.name: Specification
steps.desc: We translate the business task into clear requirements and formal specification.

steps.name: Prototype Development
steps.desc: We create a working draft solution for practical testing.

steps.name: Prototype Review
steps.desc: I present the solution, gather feedback, and clarify details.

steps.name: Main Development
steps.desc: We refine the solution and bring it to a working format.

steps.name: Final Presentation
steps.desc: I deliver the solution to the client and explain how to use it.

steps.name: Technical Support
steps.desc: After launch, I maintain the solution and help resolve issues.

steps.name: Ongoing Support
steps.desc: If needed, we continue collaboration on a regular basis.

pricing.title: Basic pricing guidelines
pricing.subtitle: Final cost depends on task scope, number of processes, detail level, and implementation format.

items.desc: Problem analysis, bottleneck identification, and recommendations.

pricing.note: Exact scope and format are discussed after a brief task diagnostic.

items.q: What companies do you work with?
items.a: I work with companies that need to organize processes, build management reporting, prepare requirements for automation, or quickly assemble an MVP. Especially useful where finance, operations, and IT intersect.

items.q: Can we start with just diagnostics?
items.a: Yes. This is often the best first step. Diagnostics helps understand the real problem, see bottlenecks, and make an informed decision about what really needs to be done next.

items.q: Is it suitable if everything is still in Excel?
items.a: Yes. This is one of the most common scenarios. We can systematize current work, build logic, automate calculations, and prepare the foundation for further growth.

items.q: What exactly is included in an MVP?
items.a: An MVP is a minimal viable solution: a working prototype with key logic that allows you to quickly test an idea and understand how the solution will work in real business conditions.

items.q: What tools do you use for solutions?
items.a: Depending on the task, it can be Excel, Google Sheets, AppSheet, Power BI, SQL, and draw.io. The tool is selected based on the real business task, timeline, and budget.

items.q: Do you only consult or implement too?
items.a: I work not only at the analysis level. When needed, I accompany the task to prototype, MVP, reporting, and working solution.

items.q: Is there support after project completion?
items.a: Yes. Technical support is included after project completion. If needed, you can switch to one-time or ongoing support.

contact.title: Describe your task — and I'll suggest a real next step
contact.subtitle: You can start with a short message: what's not working now, where the chaos is, and what result you need.
contact.messagePlaceholder: For example: need process analysis, management reporting, MVP, Excel / Google Sheets / Power BI automation
contact.success: Message sent! I'll get back to you soon.
contact.afterFormText: Usually, the first step is a short task discussion to quickly understand where the main problem is and what solution format works best.

bottomCTA.title: If there's chaos in business — it can be turned into a clear system
bottomCTA.subtitle: Start with diagnostics to see the full problem and understand which step will have maximum impact.
